summary: Crash when trying to view "Directional shadow maps" debug view

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.5.beta.double.custom_build [12dbc6681]
- Not reproducible in 4.4.1 stable
- Not reproducible in 4.5 beta 6

The way I build my version of Godot: I added `limboai` module and nothing else. I regularly rebase on the Godot master branch. I have added a build script (`UPDATE.sh`) in this branch:
https://github.com/ARez2/godot-double/tree/arez2/changes

The build command used for the sanitizer build of the fork:
```
scons platform=linuxbsd precision=double arch=x86_64 debug_symbols=yes dev_build=yes production=yes use_asan=yes use_lsan=yes use_msan=yes
```


### System information

Godot v4.5.beta.double (12dbc6681) - Ubuntu 25.04 25.04 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 7800 XT (RADV NAVI32) - AMD Ryzen 9 7900X3D 12-Core Processor (24 threads) - 61.01 GiB memory

### Issue description

summary: Multiplayer Spawner in Exports Only - The spawn function must return a Node error

description.text:
### Tested versions

-Reproducible at a minimum in 4.4.1 stable, with no separate addons loaded.


### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6950 XT (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.21025.1024) - AMD Ryzen 9 5900X 12-Core Processor (24 threads)

### Issue description

Been attempting to export a small game working on recently using GodotSteam. General scene architecture is as such upon load:
main.tscn instantiates
    lobby.tscn, which contains a child MultiplayerSpawner.
          MultiplayerSpawner uses a custom function - spawn_level(data) in order to spawn the multiplayer level for everyone upon selection.

Everything works fine within the editor, but (only with exported copy of game) attempting to click the button to move into lobby.tscn hangs the program up. The output, when ran in Debug from command line, is the following:

```
ERROR: Cannot open file 'res://Menus/lobby.tscn'.
   at: (scene/resources/resource_format_text.cpp:1388)
ERROR: Failed loading resource: res://Menus/lobby.tscn. Make sure resources have been imported by opening the project in the editor at least once.
   at: (core/io/resource_loader.cpp:343)
SCRIPT ERROR: Cannot call method 'instantiate' on a null value.
          at: spawn_level (res://menus/main.gd:16)
ERROR: The spawn function must return a Node.
   at: (modules/multiplayer/multiplayer_spawner.cpp:314)
ERROR: The 'spawn_function' callable must return a valid node.
   at: (modules/multiplayer/multiplayer_spawner.cpp:327)

```

The offending/relevant parts of code:
in lobby.gd:
```gdscript
@onready var ms = $MultiplayerSpawner

var available_levels = {
	"Housev1": "res://levels/test_level.tscn",
	"TomekHouse" : "res://levels/test_level2.tscn"
	}

```

```gdscript
func _ready() -> void:
	ms.spawn_function = spawn_level
```


Below is how the map is selected:
```gdscript
func _on_map_selected(id : int):
	var keys = available_levels.keys()
	var level = keys[id]
	var level_selected = available_levels[level]
        map_selected = level_selected
	$MapButton.text = level_selected
```

```gdscript
func _on_start_button_pressed() -> void:
	if map_selected == null:
		$"Error Messages".text = "Host Must Select Map First"
		return
#<truncated>
	ms.spawn(map_selected)
```

Below is the actual spawn_function, also in lobby.gd.

```gdscript
func spawn_level(data):
	var a = (load(data) as PackedScene).instantiate()
	return a

```

### Steps to reproduce

Difficult to say - I would assume to simply instantiate a scene that uses a custom spawn_function. Going to be doing some further testing later today to see exactly what could be causing this issue.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Definitely not a perfect MRP by any means, but unfortunately there was quite a bit of unhooking to do to remove everything from the addons I was using. I'll see if I can upload a new version later today, but this one should work fine (starting from Main.tscn -> Host LAN).

summary: [Web] ResourceLoader for background loading does not work for Web builds.

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible on: Godot 4.5 beta 6 (Standard, no GDExtensions, no C++ modules, no plugins).
- Not reproducible on: Godot 4.5 beta 5 and older Godot 4.x stable  (Standard, no GDExtensions, no C++ modules. no plugins).

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta6 - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8108) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800H with Radeon Graphics (16 threads) - 15.85 GiB memory

### Issue description

When using `ResourceLoader` to load scenes in the background (`ResourceLoader.load_threaded_request(path)`), the loading never finishes and `ResourceLoader.load_threaded_get_status` always returns `ResourceLoader.ThreadLoadStatus.THREAD_LOAD_IN_PROGRESS` and `[0.0]` progress. This only happens on web builds. 

My export presets are:

```
[preset.0]

name="Web"
platform="Web"
runnable=true
advanced_options=false
dedicated_server=false
custom_features=""
export_filter="all_resources"
include_filter=""
exclude_filter=""
export_path="_build/index.html"
patches=PackedStringArray()
encryption_include_filters=""
encryption_exclude_filters=""
seed=0
encrypt_pck=false
encrypt_directory=false
script_export_mode=2

[preset.0.options]

custom_template/debug=""
custom_template/release=""
variant/extensions_support=false
variant/thread_support=false
vram_texture_compression/for_desktop=false
vram_texture_compression/for_mobile=false
html/export_icon=true
html/custom_html_shell=""
html/head_include=""
html/canvas_resize_policy=2
html/focus_canvas_on_start=true
html/experimental_virtual_keyboard=false
progressive_web_app/enabled=false
progressive_web_app/ensure_cross_origin_isolation_headers=true
progressive_web_app/offline_page=""
progressive_web_app/display=1
progressive_web_app/orientation=0
progressive_web_app/icon_144x144=""
progressive_web_app/icon_180x180=""
progressive_web_app/icon_512x512=""
progressive_web_app/background_color=Color(0, 0, 0, 1)
threads/emscripten_pool_size=8
threads/godot_pool_size=4
```

As you can see, these are the default values for all web build options. That's why I don't think this is a duplicate of [#104498](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/104498).

For the MRP, I wrote a simple loading screen:

```gdscript
extends Control


var requested_path: String
var progress: Array[float] = []

@onready var label: Label = %Label


func _init() -> void:
	hide()


func _ready() -> void:
	process_mode = Node.PROCESS_MODE_ALWAYS
	set_process(false)


func _process(_delta: float) -> void:
	if not requested_path:
		set_process(false)
		return
	
	var status = ResourceLoader.load_threaded_get_status(requested_path, progress)
	match status:
		ResourceLoader.ThreadLoadStatus.THREAD_LOAD_IN_PROGRESS:
			label.text = "path: %s\nstatus: %s\nprogress: %s" % [requested_path, _status_to_str(status), progress]
		ResourceLoader.ThreadLoadStatus.THREAD_LOAD_LOADED:
			label.text = "path: %s\nstatus: %s\nprogress: %s" % [requested_path, _status_to_str(status), progress]
			var scene_package = ResourceLoader.load_threaded_get(requested_path)
			get_tree().root.add_child.call_deferred(scene_package.instantiate())
			requested_path = ""
			label.text = ""
			set_process(false)
			hide()
		_:
			label.text = "ERROR!"
			requested_path = ""
			set_process(false)


func request_load(path: String) -> void:
	if requested_path == path or not is_node_ready():
		return
	
	requested_path = path

	show()
	label.text = "FREEING CURRENT SCENE..."

	print("freeing current scene...")
	var curr_scene = get_tree().root.get_children()[-1]
	curr_scene.queue_free()
	while is_instance_valid(curr_scene): # The bug happens even if I remove this while.
		await get_tree().process_frame
	print("current sceene freed. Waiting for 1 sec to pass.")
	curr_scene = null
	label.text = "CURRENT SCENE FREED.\nNothing will happen for a second"
	await get_tree().create_timer(1.0).timeout
	
	var error = ResourceLoader.load_threaded_request(path)
	print("Started load of ", path)
	if error:
		label.text = "ERROR!"
		print("Error while loading ", path)
	else:
		set_process(true)


func _status_to_str(status) -> String:
	match status:
		ResourceLoader.ThreadLoadStatus.THREAD_LOAD_IN_PROGRESS:
			return "THREAD_LOAD_IN_PROGRESS"
		ResourceLoader.ThreadLoadStatus.THREAD_LOAD_LOADED:
			return "THREAD_LOAD_LOADED"
		ResourceLoader.ThreadLoadStatus.THREAD_LOAD_INVALID_RESOURCE:
			return "THREAD_LOAD_INVALID_RESOURCE"
		ResourceLoader.ThreadLoadStatus.THREAD_LOAD_FAILED:
			return "THREAD_LOAD_FAILED"
		_:
			return "UNKNOWN"
````

`_process` keeps running, so I don't thing Godot hangs while trying to load the next scene.

The same code works for Godot 4.5 beta 5 and Godot 4.x stable.

It works again If I enable variant/thread_support, but this requires enabling SharedArrayBuffer on Itch (which wasn't needed on older beta and 4.x stable versions).

### Steps to reproduce

* Use the attached MRP.
* Execute the game on a web browser with the compatibility renderer.
* Click the "Go to Scene B" button.
* A loading screen will show briefly.
* The expected result is for the loading screen to hide and the Scene B to show. Instead, this bug keeps showing the loading screen because the load status is always `ResourceLoader.ThreadLoadStatus.THREAD_LOAD_IN_PROGRESS`.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[load-threaded-web-problem.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21950762/load-threaded-web-problem.zip)

summary: Android: LightmapGI with big scenes crashes

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.5.beta5

### System information

Android (14/15)

### Issue description

Game crashes when loading a "big" scene that uses a LightmapGI node with baked lighting.
Was able to reproduce this on a 300 sqm scene.
Problem doesn't seem to be related to the lightmap texture memory footprint, as even a texture as small as 800kb (vram compressed) causes the crash.

Was able to reproduce this on Vulkan 1.3 devices (Pixel 9a w/ Android 15 and Xiaomi Redmi Note 14 Pro 5G w/ Android 15).
Vulkan 1.1 devices do not seem to suffer from this problem (Tested on Xiaomi mi 11i w/ Android 15 and Oneplus 8T w/ Android 14)

summary: Debugger: Cursor resets to window centre when using Step Into at a breakpoint (Windows, 4.4.1 stable)

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4.1.stable.official
- Not tested in earlier or dev versions. (Will update if tested in 4.3 stable or 4.5 dev snapshots)

### System information

System information  Godot v4.4.1.stable Windows 11 (build 26100) – Multi-window, 2 monitors Vulkan (Forward+) NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Laptop GPU (driver 32.0.15.8088) 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-11800H @ 2.30GHz (16 threads)

### Issue description

When the debugger pauses on a breakpoint, pressing Step Into causes the mouse cursor to snap to the centre of the running game window after each step.

This happens even though the interaction is with the debugger controls, not the game window. It makes stepping through code awkward because the cursor keeps teleporting.

Expected:
- Cursor position should remain unchanged when using debugger step controls.

Actual:
- Each Step Into (toolbar button or F11) forces the cursor to jump to the centre of the game window.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Use my attached project
2. Add a breakpoint on res://Resources/CharacterStats.gd line number 33 (while loop)
3. Run the project.
4. When execution stops at the breakpoint, press Step Into.
5. Observe: the mouse cursor snaps to the centre of the running game window.

You should go for kill using WSAD and click on right mouse button once you clicked it you just have to click on step into button for debugging.

summary: Allow extending previously-non-abstract scripts that became abstract

description.text: Fixes #109171. It is possible that the user changed an existing script to abstract after it was attached to a node. When this happens, the user needs to fix it, and we should keep track of the global class name to allow the "Extend Script" button to work. In master: The "Extend Script..." button is hidden, and an unhelpful error message is printed. It is still possible for users to workaround the issue by "Change Type..." to a class extending the abstract class, or dragging a script into the inspector. In this PR: The "Extend Script..." button appears, auto-fills with the class name, and a helpful error message is printed. This PR still isn't perfect, for example we should have some way of marking the loaded scene as changed even though the user has made no changes, but I don't know if the PackedScene class has this capability. I'm open to suggestions.

While creating a scene management system, I used script references to TSCN files to reference their entries in ResourcePreloader nodes.

summary: BlendSpaces and StateMachine grab focus too aggressively

description.text:
### Tested versions

Current master, has been a problem for as long as I can remember.

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta (28499e344) - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Single-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8097) - AMD Ryzen 9 5950X 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 63.90 GiB memory

### Issue description

When the mouse hovers over a BlendSpace1D, BlendSpace2D or StateMachine (not BlendTree), focus is immediately grabbed by them, indicated by a blue highlight in the default theme.

Not only does this not serve much of a purpose and is against editor conventions, when copying or editing text, such as blend position in a BlendSpace, or advance conditions for a transition in StateMachine, or simply typing something in the Script Editor, text editing is interrupted and leads to constant friction and misfires. I often think I've copied an advance condition for a transition to paste elsewhere but my mouse entered StateMachine's window too quickly and it stole focus before the copy.

### Steps to reproduce

- Create a project.
- Create an AnimationTree.
- Set its root node to BlendSpace1D/BlendSpace2D/StateMachine.
- Start typing text anywhere else in the editor and hover your mouse over the AnimationTree view.
- Note that typing was interrupted.

N/A

summary: BBCode 'Shake' label (maybe others) changes process rate on MacOS in editor

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.5.beta5
- MacOS, metal renderer

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta5 - macOS Tahoe (26.0.0) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M4 (Apple9) - Apple M4 (10 threads) - 16.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/08c485ff-7b13-4312-abd8-23d4b082f452

The issue, apparent in the video, happens when using a BBCode enabled RichTextLabel. If I click on any application on my second monitor on my Mac, then back on Godot,  the simulation rate / process rate of the label is SUPER fast.

It fixes when I open and close my game, and doesn't have an issue if I click on other windows on the same monitor from what I can tell, nor if I open my game. But, if I click on my web browser or Discord on my second monitor, it goes crazy again

Not a big issue, and generally when working on it I never run into it since making text is such a fast process. But, it definitely is a bug

P.S. You can also edit the text to make it go back to normal

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a BBCode Enabled rich text label in Godot 4.5.beta5
2. Add the following text/tag if you want to reproduce what I did:

```swift

[shake rate=15 level=10 connected=0][center]RELOAD[/center][/shake]

```

3. Click an application on your second monitor, causing the Godot Editor to be limited in framerate by MacOS[1]

[1]
> The framerate limitation is self-imposed by the Unfocused Low Processor Mode Sleep Usec editor setting. It's not imposed by the OS itself.
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/109879#issuecomment-3215208833

5. Click back on Godot, and watch as your `shake rate=15` looks like `shake rate=100`
6. Run a scene and close it, and it should go back to normal. Editing the text also makes it go back to normal
### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

No MRP is needed, the only requirement seems to be a RichTextLabel with BBCode (i havent tested other tags, but I assume them to be the same. At the very least, Shake does it).

1. Make a new project
2. Add a `Control` node scene
3. Add a `RichTextLabel`
4. Enabled BBCode tags
5. Paste the tag in the reproduction instructions (shake with rate of 15, level of 10, connected of 0) if you want exact reproduction

summary: SpinBox up/down arrow disabled style is not updated when using set_value_no_signal

description.text: ### Tested versions 4.4.1.stable ### System information Windows 11, Forward+ Renderer ### Issue description When a SpinBox's value is programmatically set to its min_value or max_value, the corresponding up/down arrow button should visually update to its disabled style. This works correctly when using the set_value() method. However, when using the set_value_no_signal() method, the internal value of the SpinBox is updated correctly, but the GUI does not refresh the disabled state of the arrow buttons. For example, if the value is set to max_value via set_value_no_signal(), the up-arrow remains visually enabled, even though it cannot be clicked to increase the value further. The expected behavior is that set_value_no_signal() should update the internal value and the node's own visual state, while only skipping the emission of the value_changed signal. ```gdscript extends Control @onready var spin_box: SpinBox = $SpinBox func _ready() -> void: spin_box.set_value_no_signal(100) ``` ### Steps to reproduce 1. Create a new scene with a Control node as the root. 2. Add a SpinBox node. Set its max_value to 100. 4. Attach the following GDScript to the root Control node. 5. Run the scene. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A
